title: Savory Juicy American Chicken Stew
description: A hearty, comforting dish perfect for a sick day, featuring the unique flavor of lemongrass and the warmth of bhuna spice, all within kosher-friendly guidelines. This stew is designed to be both nourishing and delicious.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chicken to the pot, cooking until it is browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove the chicken from the pot and set it aside.
Add the bruised lemongrass stalks and bhuna spice mix to the pot, cooking for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the sliced carrots and cubed potatoes, cooking for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the chicken broth, tomato paste, browned chicken, salt, and pepper to the pot. Stir well to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender.
Remove the lemongrass stalks from the pot and serve the stew hot.
